_meta.status: DRAFT — agent-researched from enrolled CD/HD drafts on data.capitol.hawaii.gov; not yet verified by Devin
caveats: Amounts from enrolled drafts, not signed GM PDFs — line-item vetoes would not show. No veto notices on the standalone bills; HB1800 (Act 175) DID get an intent-to-veto notice 6/8/26 before signing 6/26/26 — check GM1277 for budget line-item vetoes (repo post_veto CSVs may already reflect).
Session complete through ~Act 206 (last signings 7/8/26); a few late acts possible until ~7/14 — re-scrape next week.
Act 99 and Act 123 are technically supplemental/special-fund acts, not labeled one-time; included as FY27 analogues of last year's list.

branches_fy2027._comment: FY27 amounts = 2025 biennium acts as amended by 2026 session. Extracted from enrolled texts (data.capitol.hawaii.gov mirror) and GM PDFs.

judiciary.acts: Act 227, SLH 2025 as amended by Act 178, SLH 2026 (HB2095 CD1, Judiciary Supplemental Appropriations Act of 2026)
judiciary.cip_note: GO bonds; Act 178 raised biennium CIP from $12.9M to $58.3M ($45.4M is FY27: Kauai chillers $1.2M, Hale Kaulike AC $1.2M, Kaahumanu Hale elevators $15M, lump sum $8M, South Kohala courthouse $20M)
judiciary.emergency_note: No FY27 judiciary emergency appropriation found in 2026 session (FY26 edition had $4.7M)

legislature.acts: Act 001, SLH 2026 (HB2240 HD1) + Act 127, SLH 2025 (HB1439 CD1) FY27 cost items. NOTE: FY26 primer endnote 5 says 'Act 27' but links GM1227 which is Act 127 — label typo in original.
legislature.methodology_note: Published-style ≈ Act1 GF ($43,068,647 incl $2.8M RF deposit) + $6.8M audit RF spend + Act 127 FY27 cost items ($2,030,952). FY26 published $51.63M reproduces to within ~$0.3M under this method (Act1-2025 $44.02M + $6.8M + cost-item subset). DEVIN TO CONFIRM inclusion rules — RF deposit+spend double-counts $2.8M.

oha.acts: Act 248, SLH 2025 (HB410 CD1, OHA Appropriations Act) — FY27 column; NO 2026 act amends it
oha.one_time_note: Act 032, SLH 2026 (SB903) additionally makes a one-time $55M public-land-trust transfer to OHA — listed under one_time_fy2027.featured; FY26 edition kept OHA column at operating $6M only

emergency_fy2027.purpose: Collective bargaining cost items for Bargaining Unit 11 and excluded counterparts — salaries and health premiums, 2025-27 biennium

emergency_fy2026_passed_2026.purpose: Backfill DHS general funds redirected to emergency food assistance during the 2025 federal government shutdown
BU-1 and BU-10 labor grievance cost items incl. DOE temporary hazard pay ($66.8M GF + $28.5M GF DOE + minor funds); extends Act 29 SLH 2025 lapse to 6/30/27

featured.detail: $600M mass transit special fund (county-surcharge disbursements, HRS §248-2); $100M GF to new major disaster fund (+$100M appropriated out); ops: ADC $4,762,987, HTDC $2,592,854, HCDA $984,450
featured.purpose: Transit funding and disaster recovery/response reserve

featured.detail: $146M King Kamehameha III Elementary rebuild (contingent on ≥$48M federal match) + $30M offsite infrastructure + $12M roadways + $12M Lahaina small boat harbor — from state risk management revolving fund
featured.purpose: Distribute 2023 Maui wildfire property/liability insurance proceeds

featured.detail: Explicit one-time transfer re: public land trust revenues; reconstitutes PLT working group
featured.purpose: One-time transfer to OHA for public land trust revenues

featured.detail: $49.5M stadium development special fund + $728,927 GF and 3 FTEs
featured.purpose: Halawa Community Development District / New Aloha Stadium

featured.detail: Revenue bond proceeds; authorizes new revenue bonds
General funds, FY27, effective 7/1/26 (non-emergency part of the act)
featured.purpose: Supplement premium contributions to health plans for residents losing ACA coverage due to federal policy changes

featured.detail: §4 $2,800,000 GF FY27 meal subsidies for students ≤300% FPL (ALICE expansion, 2026-27 school year) + §3 $565,000 FY27 reduced-price-eligible students
featured.purpose: Free school meals expansion to ALICE households — the FY27 follow-on the FY26 primer teased

mid_size.purpose: DOCARE Hawaii Island patrol vessel + 20 vehicles
Office of the State Fire Marshal (also $2,212,000 in FY26)
Medicaid home/community-based residential service rates (federal match required)
Care home resilience pilot, Central/Leeward Oahu

small.purpose: Community behavioral health clinics certifying office

excluded.reason: Routine annual legislative-agency appropriations (Auditor, LRB, Ombudsman, Ethics) — FY26 primer did not count the 2025 equivalent as one-time; belongs in Legislature branch column instead
